code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about athletes into a single fluent sentence.
    All triples are rendered in one clause, using simple predicate‑to‑phrase
    templates. The subject is prefixed to each clause to keep the meaning
    clear, and the clauses are joined with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Template mapping for known predicates
    templates = {
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "height": "is {obj} meters tall",
        "weight": "weighs {obj}",
        "club": "plays for {obj}",
        "currentclub": "currently plays for {obj}",
        "currentteam": "currently plays for {obj}",
        "draftTeam": "was drafted by {obj}",
        "draftYear": "was drafted in {obj}",
        "draftRound": "was drafted in round {obj}",
        "draftPick": "was the {obj} pick in the draft",
        "debutTeam": "debuted for {obj}",
        "position": "plays as {obj}",
        "league": "competes in the {obj} league",
        "manager": "is managed by {obj}",
        "ground": "has home ground {obj}",
        "coach": "is coached by {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "chairman": "is chaired by {obj}",
        "generalManager": "has general manager {obj}",
        "youthclub": "started at youth club {obj}",
        "college": "attended {obj}",
        "playerNumber": "wears number {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started his professional career in {obj}",
        "formerTeam": "formerly played for {obj}",
        "season": "played in the {obj} season",
        "city": "is based in {obj}",
        "country": "is from {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the {obj} currency",
        "demonym": "are called {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "belongs to the {obj} ethnic group",
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset {obj}",
        "timeZone": "lies in the {obj} time zone",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "mostChampions": "has the most championships: {obj}",
        "stadium": "plays at {obj}",
        "universityTeam": "represents {obj}",
        "season": "participated in {obj}",
    }

    # Build a flat list of clause strings
    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause_body = tmpl.format(obj=t.object)
        else:
            # fallback: turn predicate into readable words
            pred = t.predicate.replace("_", " ")
            clause_body = f"{pred} {t.object}"
        clauses.append(f"{t.subject} {clause_body}")

    # Join clauses into one sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    # Capitalize first character and ensure a period at the end
    sentence = sentence.strip()
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
